method_atom: a semiclassical wave-packet treatment of higher-order solutions of a wave equation that carry a quantized topological circulation (a phase vortex) and thus a well-defined internal angular momentum aligned with the propagation direction, so that the probability/energy current coils around the momentum. A geometric (Berry) curvature appears in the conjugate momentum space with the structure of a magnetic-monopole field, producing an anomalous transverse (Magnus-type) force and a spin-orbit-like coupling between the internal circulation and the packet's center-of-mass motion, which drives a topological transverse-current (Hall-type) effect; an external field can additionally couple to the internal angular momentum like a Zeeman term.
object_atom: electron wave packets governed by the Schrodinger equation, moving in external electric and magnetic fields.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the semiclassical dynamics of a vortex-carrying wave packet with monopole-like Berry curvature yielding an anomalous transverse force and Hall effect; the electron obeying the Schrodinger equation in electromagnetic fields is the physics-specific object, and the two separate cleanly.
